steps.step: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of \(9x^6\) and \(63x^2\). The GCF of 9 and 63 is 9, and the GCF of \(x^6\) and \(x^2\) is \(x^2\), so the overall GCF is \(9x^2\).
Factor \(9x^2\) out of the expression: \(9x^6 - 63x^2 = 9x^2 \cdot x^4 - 9x^2 \cdot 7 = 9x^2(x^4 - 7)\).
Verify by expanding: \(9x^2 \cdot x^4 = 9x^6\) and \(9x^2 \cdot 7 = 63x^2\), so \(9x^2(x^4-7) = 9x^6 - 63x^2\). Confirmed.

• Choice A: \(9(x^3-7x^2)^2\). Expanding gives \(9(x^6 - 14x^5 + 49x^4)\), which introduces \(x^5\) and \(x^4\) terms not in the original. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: \(x^2(9x-7)^4\). Raising \((9x-7)\) to the fourth power yields a polynomial with five terms; multiplied by \(x^2\) this cannot simplify to just two terms. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: \(9x^2(x-7)^4\). Expanding \((x-7)^4\) gives \(x^4 - 28x^3 + 294x^2 - 1372x + 2401\); after multiplying by \(9x^2\) there are far more terms than the original expression. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: \(9x^2(x^4-7) = 9x^6 - 63x^2\). Matches exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
